Value: Unit: nan, Batch x1: nan, Batch x2: nan, Batch x3: nan, Batch x4: nan, Prep/Brand:
Peel the skin of the daikon and cut the radish to the width of a sheet of nori (futomaki style)
Using a Katsuramaki Turning Slicer (Japanese Vegetable Sheeter), attach the radish piece to the designated pins
Begin spinning the hand spinner first, and use the push bar to bring the radish up to the moving blade
Begin applying more pressure as the radish starts to cut, until unnevenness is fixed and the sheet is contiguous
Remove the sheeted radish and cut into 10 inch lengths
Stack sheets together and reserve in a container in water
Change water up to twice daily, or any time the radish is noticably pungeant when container is opened
